description_long: This is a single white calla lily flower with a long, slender green stem and a distinctive trumpet-shaped spathe. The spathe is smooth, gently flaring outward at the top, and curves elegantly, forming a funnel that partially encloses a central yellow spadix. The flower's surface appears soft and delicate, with subtle gradients of white and hints of green near the base. The stem is cylindrical, straight, and uniform in color, providing a striking contrast to the white bloom. No leaves are visible, and the overall form is minimalist and elegant, typical of ornamental flowers found in urban parks or gardens.
description: A white calla lily flower with a long green stem and a smooth, trumpet-shaped bloom surrounding a yellow spadix. The design is elegant and minimal, with no visible leaves.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open trumpet-shaped spathe and the central yellow spadix clearly.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the gentle outward curve of the spathe and the elongated stem.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the spathe and the full length of the green stem.
description_view_3: Angled side view emphasizing the asymmetrical flare of the spathe and the smooth transition to the stem.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The flower has a single, large, funnel-shaped spathe that is wrapped around the central spadix, forming a bilateral arrangement with a smooth, continuous surface and no visible separation between petals.
